description: Brands offer product samples to followers and request honest ratings or feedback, creating interactive engagement through consumer trials and authentic peer reviews.
structural_recipe: Post a product sample or trial offering with a call-to-action requesting direct, honest feedback. Include a rating scale (e.g., 'من 10؟'), encourage truthful responses using colloquial Arabic phrases like 'خلك صريح' (be honest), and tag the brand. Create a conversational tone that normalizes candid criticism rather than just praise.
why_it_works: Saudi consumers value authenticity and peer recommendations highly. By explicitly requesting honest feedback ('خلك صريح'), brands build trust and reduce skepticism about sponsored content. The interactive rating format gamifies participation, while the invitation for sincere critique appeals to the Saudi cultural value of straightforwardness in trusted circles.
transplantation_caveats: Requires authentic follow-through on negative feedback; ignoring critical comments undermines the pattern
May not work if audience perceives brand cannot handle criticism
Regulatory considerations around product sampling claims and disclosure requirements
